tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:o-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-macosx10.10.0"

define i1 @test12(i32 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @test12(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ZEXT:%.*]] = zext i32 [[X:%.*]] to i64
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[MUL:%.*]] = mul nuw nsw i64 [[ZEXT]], 7
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SHR:%.*]] = lshr i64 [[MUL]], 32
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TRUNC:%.*]] = trunc nuw nsw i64 [[SHR]] to i32
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 true
  %zext = zext i32 %x to i64
  %mul = mul nuw i64 %zext, 7
  %shr = lshr i64 %mul, 32
  %trunc = trunc i64 %shr to i32
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %trunc, 7
  ret i1 %cmp
}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

  %zext = zext i32 %x to i64
  %mul = mul nuw i64 %zext, 7
  %shr = lshr i64 %mul, 32
  %trunc = trunc i64 %shr to i32
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %trunc, 7
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @ctlz_with_range_metadata(i16 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @ctlz_with_range_metadata(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CTLZ:%.*]] = call i16 @llvm.ctlz.i16(i16 [[X:%.*]], i1 false), !range [[RNG5:![0-9]+]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TRUNC:%.*]] = trunc nuw nsw i16 [[CTLZ]] to i8
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 true
  %ctlz = call i16 @llvm.ctlz.i16(i16 %x, i1 false), !range !0
  %trunc = trunc i16 %ctlz to i8
  %res = icmp ult i8 %trunc, 8
  ret i1 %res
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i16 @llvm.ctlz.i16(i16, i1 immarg) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

!0 = !{i16 0, i16 8}

; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ABS:%.*]] = call i16 @llvm.abs.i16(i16 [[X:%.*]], i1 false), !range [[RNG5]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TRUNC:%.*]] = trunc nuw nsw i16 [[ABS]] to i8
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 true
  %abs = call i16 @llvm.abs.i16(i16 %x, i1 false), !range !0
  %trunc = trunc i16 %abs to i8
  %res = icmp ult i8 %trunc, 8
  ret i1 %res
}

declare void @use64(i64)

define void @test4(i32 %n) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @test4(
; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp sgt i32 [[N:%.*]], -2
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[CMP]], label [[BB:%.*]], label [[EXIT:%.*]]
; CHECK:       bb:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[EXT_WIDE:%.*]] = sext i32 [[N]] to i64
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use64(i64 [[EXT_WIDE]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[EXT:%.*]] = trunc nsw i64 [[EXT_WIDE]] to i32
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[EXIT]]
; CHECK:       exit:
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %n, -2
  br i1 %cmp, label %bb, label %exit

bb:                                               ; preds = %entry
  %ext.wide = sext i32 %n to i64
  call void @use64(i64 %ext.wide)
  %ext = trunc i64 %ext.wide to i32
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %bb, %entry
  ret void
}

tests.test_body:
define i1 @rust_issue_122734(i8 range(i8 0, 3) %A, i8 range(i8 0, 3) %B) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define i1 @rust_issue_122734(
; CHECK-SAME: i8 range(i8 0, 3) [[A:%.*]], i8 range(i8 0, 3) [[B:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:  [[START:.*]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[LHS:%.*]] = icmp eq i8 [[A]], 2
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[RHS:%.*]] = icmp eq i8 [[B]], 2
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[OR:%.*]] = or i1 [[LHS]], [[RHS]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[AND:%.*]] = and i1 [[LHS]], [[RHS]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[OR]], label %[[IFTRUE:.*]], label %[[IFFALSE:.*]]
; CHECK:       [[IFTRUE]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[PHI:%.*]] = phi i1 [ [[XOR2:%.*]], %[[IFFALSE]] ], [ [[AND]], %[[START]] ]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[PHI]]
; CHECK:       [[IFFALSE]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XOR:%.*]] = xor i8 [[A]], [[B]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TRUNC:%.*]] = trunc nuw i8 [[XOR]] to i1
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XOR2]] = xor i1 [[TRUNC]], true
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label %[[IFTRUE]]
start:
  %lhs = icmp eq i8 %A, 2
  %rhs = icmp eq i8 %B, 2
  %or = or i1 %lhs, %rhs
  %and = and i1 %lhs, %rhs
  br i1 %or, label %iftrue, label %iffalse

iftrue:                                           ; preds = %iffalse, %start
  %phi = phi i1 [ %xor2, %iffalse ], [ %and, %start ]
  ret i1 %phi

iffalse:                                          ; preds = %start
  %xor = xor i8 %A, %B
  %trunc = trunc i8 %xor to i1
  %xor2 = xor i1 %trunc, true
  br label %iftrue
}
tests.source_program:
define i1 @rust_issue_122734(i8 range(i8 0, 3) %A, i8 range(i8 0, 3) %B) {
start:
  %lhs = icmp eq i8 %A, 2
  %rhs = icmp eq i8 %B, 2
  %or = or i1 %lhs, %rhs
  %and = and i1 %lhs, %rhs
  br i1 %or, label %iftrue, label %iffalse

iftrue:                                           ; preds = %iffalse, %start
  %phi = phi i1 [ %xor2, %iffalse ], [ %and, %start ]
  ret i1 %phi

iffalse:                                          ; preds = %start
  %xor = xor i8 %A, %B
  %trunc = trunc i8 %xor to i1
  %xor2 = xor i1 %trunc, true
  br label %iftrue
}

define i8 @infer_nsw_from_assume(i16 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define i8 @infer_nsw_from_assume(
; CHECK-SAME: i16 [[X:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ADD:%.*]] = add i16 [[X]], 128
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[OR_COND_I:%.*]] = icmp ult i16 [[ADD]], 256
; CHECK-NEXT:    tail call void @llvm.assume(i1 [[OR_COND_I]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CONV1:%.*]] = trunc nsw i16 [[X]] to i8
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i8 [[CONV1]]
  %add = add i16 %x, 128
  %or.cond.i = icmp ult i16 %add, 256
  tail call void @llvm.assume(i1 %or.cond.i)
  %conv1 = trunc i16 %x to i8
  ret i8 %conv1
}

issue.title: [CVP] Failure to recognise trunc nsw from assumptions
issue.body:
Pulled out of #130088
```ll
----------------------------------------
define i8 @src(i16 noundef %x) {
#0:
  %add = add i16 noundef %x, 128
  %or.cond.i = icmp ult i16 %add, 256
  assume i1 %or.cond.i
  %conv1 = trunc i16 noundef %x to i8
  ret i8 %conv1
}
=>
define i8 @tgt(i16 noundef %x) {
#0:
  %conv1 = trunc nsw i16 noundef %x to i8
  ret i8 %conv1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
```
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/2qhZy4
issue.author: RKSimon

comments.author: bababuck
comments.body: @dtcxzyw Can iI give this a go if no one else is already working on it?

comments.author: ArunaHulakoti
comments.body: @llvmbot  Could you please provide more details on the expected  behavior?

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
> [@dtcxzyw](https://github.com/dtcxzyw) Can iI give this a go if no one else is already working on it?

Sorry to tell you that this issue is covered by https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/130504.

comments.author: veera-sivarajan
comments.body: @bababuck sorry, I didn't know you were assigned to this.
